VAL-MAP-001.title: Map loads centered on Bushwick with dark theme

steps.expected: Page loads with MapLibre GL map
steps.observed: Page loads, MapLibre map DOM elements mount (.maplibregl-map, .maplibregl-canvas exist). Map instance fails to initialize due to WebGL context creation error (SwiftShader Vulkan driver).

steps.action: Verify map center coordinates via React props
steps.observed: React Map component props: latitude=40.6944, longitude=-73.9213 — correct Bushwick coordinates

steps.action: Verify zoom level via React props
steps.observed: React Map component props: zoom=15 — correct

steps.action: Verify dark color scheme via map style JSON
steps.expected: Deep purple/black background, no white areas
steps.observed: Map style background-color=#1D1D3A (Deep Purple), main element computed bg=rgb(29,29,58) — correct dark theme. All land/water/park layers use dark colors.

Map style source URL=https://tiles.openfreemap.org/planet. curl to tiles.openfreemap.org/planet returns HTTP 200. However, no tile requests were made in browser because map instance failed to initialize (WebGL unavailable).

evidence.consoleErrors: WebGL context creation error: SwiftShader Vulkan driver cannot create WebGL context. This is a known headless Chrome environment limitation, not a code bug.
evidence.network: No tile requests made (map instance null due to WebGL failure). curl to tiles.openfreemap.org/planet returns 200.

VAL-MAP-001.issues: Map instance is null because WebGL is unavailable in headless Chrome (SwiftShader GPU failure). Tile requests were never made. Configuration is correct but runtime rendering cannot be verified. This is a known environment limitation per AGENTS.md.

VAL-MAP-002.title: 3D building extrusions visible on initial load

steps.action: Verify pitch setting via React props
steps.observed: React Map component props: pitch=50 — within expected range

steps.action: Verify 3D building extrusion layer exists in map style
steps.observed: Layer 'building-3d-dark' exists with type='fill-extrusion', source-layer='building', minzoom=14. Uses render_height for extrusion height and render_min_height for base.

steps.action: Verify building colors use EVA palette (not default gray)
steps.expected: Dark fills with EVA color accents
steps.observed: fill-extrusion-color interpolates: height 0→#0a0a2e (dark navy), 15→#2a1a4e (dark purple), 40→#765898 (medium purple). Opacity 0.85. These are EVA palette colors, not default gray.

VAL-MAP-002.issues: Cannot visually verify 3D building rendering due to WebGL unavailability. Configuration is correct: pitch=50, fill-extrusion layer exists with EVA colors. Known environment limitation.

VAL-MAP-003.title: Camera pitch and rotate controls

steps.expected: Right-click drag changes pitch and bearing
steps.observed: Map component has maxPitch=85, initial pitch=50, bearing=-15. No dragRotate or dragPitch disabled props. However, map instance is null so interaction cannot be tested.

steps.action: Attempt to interact with map via right-click drag
steps.observed: Cannot test — map instance is null (WebGL unavailable), so mouse interactions have no effect on map state.

VAL-MAP-003.issues: Cannot test camera pitch/rotate interactions because map instance is null (WebGL unavailable). Configuration allows pitch up to 85° and does not disable drag-rotate. Known environment limitation.

VAL-MAP-004.title: Zoom controls work

steps.action: Verify zoom bounds via React props
Verify NavigationControl component in source code
steps.expected: Zoom +/- buttons rendered with EVA styling
steps.observed: NavigationControl component is rendered at position='bottom-right' with showCompass=false. Custom CSS (map-controls.css) applies EVA theme: dark background rgba(29,29,58,0.85), NERV Orange border (#F0903A), neon glow box-shadow, clip-path polygon corners.

steps.action: Check DOM for zoom control elements
steps.observed: maplibregl-ctrl-bottom-right container exists but is EMPTY. Zoom buttons (.maplibregl-ctrl-zoom-in, .maplibregl-ctrl-zoom-out) do NOT exist in DOM. Controls failed to render because map instance is null.

VAL-MAP-004.issues: Zoom controls did not render because map instance is null (WebGL unavailable). The NavigationControl component IS configured in source code with EVA theme CSS, and zoom bounds (12-18) are correctly set. Cannot verify runtime behavior. Known environment limitation.

VAL-MAP-005.title: Pan and navigation controls

steps.observed: No dragPan disabled prop in Map component. However, map instance is null so pan cannot be tested.

steps.action: Verify zoom controls styled in EVA theme
steps.expected: Visible zoom +/- controls in EVA theme colors
steps.observed: NavigationControl configured at bottom-right. CSS file applies EVA styling (NERV Orange border, dark bg, neon glow, clip-path polygon). Controls did not render because map instance is null.

VAL-MAP-005.issues: Cannot test pan interaction or verify control rendering because map instance is null (WebGL unavailable). Source code correctly configures NavigationControl with EVA theme CSS at bottom-right position. Known environment limitation.

VAL-MAP-006.title: Dark cyberpunk map style applied

steps.action: Verify road colors in map style JSON
steps.expected: Neon EVA palette colors (NERV Orange, MAGI Blue, Entry Plug Green)
steps.observed: road_trunk_primary=#F0903A (NERV Orange), road_secondary_tertiary=#54A2D4 (MAGI Blue), road_minor=rgba(84,162,212,0.4) (MAGI Blue 40%), road_motorway=#F0903A (NERV Orange). NOT default white/gray.
park fill-color=#0a2a1a (dark green) with outline rgba(88,242,165,0.15) — correct
All label layers use ['Share Tech Mono Regular', 'Noto Sans Regular'] — monospace technical font. Label color=#58F2A5 (Entry Plug Green). Water labels=#54A2D4 (MAGI Blue).

VAL-MAP-006.issues: Cannot visually verify rendered map style because WebGL is unavailable. Map style JSON configuration is verified correct: roads use NERV Orange/MAGI Blue, water is dark blue/black, parks are dark green, labels use Share Tech Mono monospace font in Entry Plug Green. Known environment limitation.

frictions.description: WebGL is unavailable in headless Chrome (SwiftShader Vulkan driver failure). MapLibre GL map instance is null — the map mounts DOM elements but cannot initialize its WebGL painter. This prevents all tile loading, rendering, and control initialization.
frictions.resolution: Verified map configuration via React component props and map style JSON instead of runtime map state. All assertions are blocked for visual rendering but configuration is confirmed correct.

frictions.description: NavigationControl zoom buttons did not render in DOM because they depend on a functioning map instance. The maplibregl-ctrl-bottom-right container exists but is empty.
frictions.resolution: Verified via source code that NavigationControl is configured with position='bottom-right' and EVA theme CSS is applied in map-controls.css.

blockers.description: WebGL context creation fails in headless Chrome: 'Could not create a WebGL context, VENDOR = 0xffff, DEVICE = 0xffff, GL_VENDOR = Google Inc. (Google), GL_RENDERER = ANGLE (Google, Vulkan 1.3.0 (SwiftShader Device), SwiftShader driver-5.0.0)'. MapLibre GL JS requires WebGL to initialize the map. The map instance (accessed via react-map-gl internal state) is null.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: This is a known environment limitation documented in AGENTS.md. No fix possible without GPU/WebGL support in the testing environment. Used React props and map style JSON verification as alternative evidence.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 6 blocked (all blocked by WebGL unavailability in headless Chrome). All map configuration is verified correct via React component props and map style JSON: center=40.6944/-73.9213 (Bushwick), zoom=15, pitch=50, minZoom=12, maxZoom=18, dark EVA palette colors throughout (roads=#F0903A/#54A2D4, water=#001a2c, parks=#0a2a1a, labels=Share Tech Mono in #58F2A5), 3D building extrusion layer with EVA color interpolation. The MapLibre map instance is null due to WebGL context creation failure (SwiftShader), preventing tile loading, rendering, control initialization, and interaction testing.
